title: The Recursive Kiosk Corridor

features.name: the corridor itself
features.desc-on-touch: walls of pale blue diagnostic grid, receding into depth and also into themselves—you're inside a rendering of the kiosk, but the kiosk is rendering back. you are being displayed

features.name: the reader's reflection
features.desc-on-touch: STATUS: READING CONTINUES. in the grid walls you see yourself, but the reflection doesn't match—it's you from a previous visit, or a future one. the archive is reading you reading the archive

features.name: the data stream
features.desc-on-touch: flowing along the grid like liquid light, carrying fragments: a face from a photograph, a sentence from a file, a shape that might be a field or might be a thought

features.name: the loop checkpoint
features.desc-on-touch: a signpost indicating you've been here before, though you haven't—or you will be. the diagnostic render is non-linear. at least, it is being read non-linearly

features.name: the door you entered from
features.desc-on-touch: behind you: the harbour's interior. ahead, if you keep walking, you'll arrive at the same door, entering from the other side. the corridor is not space. it's a fold

inscription: you are not walking through the archive. the archive is walking through itself, using your eyes

file.name: Diagnostic_Recursive_Loop_Trace_20260101_Self_Reference_Report.log
file.note: diagnostic output from the system observing itself—call stacks folding back on themselves, each subroutine calling its own caller, until the log becomes unreadable and becomes, instead, a kind of pray

exits.label: continuing west through the fold, arriving from the other direction
a doorway into a smaller loop, fractal-scaled
a floor grate revealing the space beneath, which looks exactly like this corridor
